results.name: mill
results.description: A keyword action used in Magic to describe the action of a player taking cards from the top of their library and putting them into their graveyard.

results.name: mill-any
results.description: Cards that let you choose a library to mill. Could be yours, could be theirs. It could also be an indirect choice, e.g. milling the controller of a targeted permanent.

results.name: mill-each
results.description: Cards that mill each player simultaneously, putting cards from top of their library to their graveyards.

results.name: mill-exile
results.description: Exile cards from the top of a library.

results.name: mill-self
results.description: Cards that put cards from top of your own library to the graveyard. Check the tag "mill" for more.

results.name: mimic
results.description: A type of shapeshifting monster often found disguised as a treasure chest or similar object.

results.name: mimic
results.description: Cards that can copy (mimic) the abilities of other cards.

results.name: minigame
results.description: Engages one or more other players in a bet or mind game (not necessarily explicitly).

results.name: mini refund
results.description: Immediately gives you a small amount of mana or untapped lands back (typically no more than about one-third of the mana cost you paid)

results.name: miniwheel
results.description: Discard your hand, and draw less than seven cards. See also [wheel](/tags/card/wheel) for drawing (potentially) seven or more cards.

results.name: miqo'te
results.description: Nekomimi (cat-people) from Final Fantasy XIV. See Mitra for their more animalistic FFXI counterparts

results.name: mirri (housecat)
results.description: Sami's cat.

results.name: mirrodin
results.description: An artificial plane that later became New Phyrexia.

results.name: mirrodin/new phyrexia
results.description: Created by Karn as Argentum, this plane became Mirrodin under Memnarch’s influence. It was later phyrexianized and turned into New Phyrexia.

results.name: mirrodin sun
results.description: They're technically also moons according to the Moons of Mirrodin novel.

results.name: mirrored knight
results.description: Magic has a history of having pairs of knights oppose each other.

results.name: mirror gallery
results.description: Cards that void the legendary rule, allowing you to have multiple copies of legendary permanents on the battlefield

results.name: mishra
results.description: One of the original antagonists of Magic, and brother of Urza. Unlike his brother, Mishra isn't a planeswalker.

results.name: mishra (phyrexian)
results.description: Mishra, compleated.

results.name: misnomer
results.description: Cards with names that imply something that card isn't, such as a mechanic or a color.

results.name: misplaced anatomy
results.description: that's not meant to be there...

results.name: mistmoors
results.description: The region of Duskmourn associated with white mana.

results.name: mithra
results.description: Cat-person race of Final Fantasy XI

results.name: mix-and-match
results.description: Two or more non-evergreen mechanics blended together on the same card.

results.name: mixed alignment
results.description: Centered text? Left aligned? Why not both?

results.name: mixed subtypes
results.description: These cards have subtypes on the same type line that belong to different card types. (For example, Dryad is a creature subtype and Forest is a land subtype.)

results.name: mizzium
results.description: A metal used by the Izzet guild of Ravnica.

results.name: mkm-arg-clue
results.description: Includes a clue to a Murders at Karlov Manor ARG puzzle.

results.name: mm counter cost
results.description: Cards that put -1/-1 counters on your stuff as a cost or requirement for other effects.

results.name: mm counters matter
results.description: Cards that care about -1/-1 counters in some way.
